title: For the Friend Who Reads With You
scripture_text: Two are better than one, because they have a good return for their labor.
reflection: The book is half the gift. The friend is the other half. Reading together is one of the holiest things two people can do.
body:
You are in a coffee shop with a friend, and you are reading. The same book, separately. Or different books, with the same quiet.

This is prayer. Or close to it. The quiet attention to a page is its own form of attention to the world, to the soul, to the Lord.

The friend is not saying much. The friend is not saying anything. The friend is just there, in the chair, in the warm room, in the afternoon. That is enough. That is the whole of the friendship.

The Lord is in the books. He is in the quiet. He is in the friend. He is in the afternoon. Be here.
prayer: Lord, thank You for this friend. For books. For quiet. For coffee. Amen.
